description: Young soybeans harvested before full ripening, usually boiled or steamed and served in the pod with salt. They are a simple snack or side dish common in Japanese cuisine.
preparation_steps: Boil or steam the edamame until tender.
Drain and season lightly, often with salt.
Serve warm or at room temperature, usually in the pod.
Squeeze the beans from the pod before eating.

health_advice.benefits: A good plant-based source of protein and fiber and can be a filling snack.
health_advice.risks: May not suit people with soy allergy; salted versions can also be high in sodium.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Choose lightly salted edamame to keep sodium more moderate.

dietary_notes.note: Naturally vegan and gluten-free when served simply.
